item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a fair critique. The main idea that I was going for is that the magma/lava is "organic" material. The inner core of the planet is spatially distinct from the crust and the outer core. The whole idea is that the inner workings of the planet exist on a level of cosmic contradiction that barely makes sense.

The idea behind the "heart" is a little unclear so thats a fair critique. I was going for the idea that the "heart" is some kind of bizarre physical core of the skip, while the whole "core" it inhabits isn't quite within this dimensional plane.

All in all a lot of this is a fair criticism, there are definitely elements of the skip that could be better. I'm not familiar with the pluto one though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The core concept here is almost exactly like SCP-2362, just with Earth instead of Pluto.

Critique collapsed to save page space
these are words

My main problem with this is an entirely mundane phenomenon that is extremely well-documented and entirely reliant on the Earth's composition being what mainstream science says it is: volcanoes.
If the mantle is made of organic material…where does magma/lava come from?

I'm also unclear on the exact nature of the place the "dimensional rifts" lead to. -1A and -1B are said to lead to the core, but the temporary portals are mentioned as showing stars and nebulae…but then portions of the Site lost to a temporary portal are visible through -1A. The letter from the dead guy also mentions starlight. So are there stars/nebluae (or things that look like them) in the core area?

The "heart" aspect is also unclear. Is it part of the embryo (as in its actual heart)? It's mentioned as being "known to be a physical avatar for SCP-3805"…but a non-physical aspect isn't ever mentioned, so I'm not sure what that is supposed to mean. It just makes the nature of the thing more confusing.

All in all, currently downvoting because major aspects of the phenomenon are contradictory or are otherwise unclear when it seems it was intended to be clear(er).

(And to be fair, I'm not a fan of the Pluto one either.)

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Here are links for easier accessibility:

Image One

Image Two

item.title: Re: Really liked it ,+1 for sure
Really liked it ,+1 for sure
item.content:encoded:
I really liked it, it's very well made, some error here and there but they are fine, the ideia itself is incredibly cool and execution is awesome. Ritual is interesting bad very dark and heavy. +1 for sure.

My recommendation is adding more addendums or explaining more of the ones already existing ( maybe explaining the comet addendum) or one of the main thing I didn't like , which is redacting the exploration log.

I was really interested ( even if it makes sense in the Canon) so maybe making it like level 4 or level 5 restricted makes more sense and writing in it something really important that would make it classified.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So Earth is a voidspawn? Can't wait for it to spawn and ride us all to victory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept and article are both pretty great, but there was a lot of "it's" that should be "its". I'm upvoting, but those mistakes need fixin'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the corrections. I'm sticking with the current term because it's in essence the most straighforward way to explain what would otherwise be needlessly complex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like what's going on here but there is a capitalization error in the stimuli log and I would choose a different term other than "dimensional rift". Also and don't think "tied" needs to be in quotations.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Sorry for the mistagging, I tried to label the skip based on the tag guide. Thanks for the heads up though.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Author, I've cleared the tags. If you're not going to add the correct the tags for your article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding the incorrect tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.
